jokes.text: You could go back and forth the whole parade.
jokes.setup: Elaborate illusion about switching sexualities in closets during a parade
jokes.punchline: Casual suggestion to keep switching back and forth, reducing profound identity change to a carnival ride
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob logic - treating profound personal transformation as casual entertainment, delivered with characteristic obliviousness

jokes.text: And breathe a word about this to anybody and you're fired.
jokes.setup: Gob celebrating that Tony Wonder is in for the illusion
jokes.punchline: Immediately threatens to fire people for talking about the very thing he just celebrated publicly
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob contradiction - celebrating and then threatening secrecy in the same breath

jokes.text: Well, I'm not going to be the one who apologizes, if that's what you're waiting for. - Well, I'm sorry, but... - Apology accepted.
jokes.setup: Lucille declares she won't apologize
jokes.punchline: Immediately accepts Buster's inadvertent 'I'm sorry' as the apology she just refused to give
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille manipulation - twisting language to get what she wants while maintaining her position. Excellent structure with immediate reversal.

jokes.text: Did I exaggerate about her? She really is just like the mother in Two and a Half Men.
jokes.setup: Buster talking about watching TV in prison
jokes.punchline: Comparing Lucille to a sitcom mother while being IN a sitcom
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about sitcom mothers while the character is unknowingly in a sitcom with an extreme mother figure

jokes.text: There's no scary half-man that I couldn't handle.
jokes.setup: Buster being proud of overcoming TV fears
jokes.punchline: Buster, who's terrified of everything, claiming he could handle something scary
jokes.explanation: Character-specific irony - Buster claiming bravery when he's famously cowardly

jokes.text: I touched a mouse. I read an illustrated Qur'an for kids, and at recess once, I sat in 'Blacks only.' Yard. Oh, I keep doing that.
jokes.setup: Buster listing rebellious prison activities
jokes.punchline: Escalating from innocent to accidentally problematic, with self-correction showing his obliviousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster escalation from innocent to problematic, with the 'I keep doing that' showing his innocent confusion about language

jokes.text: You'll need the warden's permission, and then you'll have to sponsor me for my furlough, and they probably won't let me on the float again, but that's just... Oh, God, it's still the parade, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Buster negotiating his mother's bribery
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's still obsessed with the parade despite claiming maturity
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Buster's attempt at adult negotiation dissolving into childhood parade obsession

jokes.text: Well, it's either that, or I'm gonna start spewing it.
jokes.setup: Discussing Buster picking up trash vs. having his say
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'spewing trash' literally and figuratively
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay connecting literal trash pickup to metaphorical trash talk

jokes.text: Half-man? Jon Cryer. Oh.
jokes.setup: Discussing the 'half-man' from Two and a Half Men
jokes.punchline: Confusion over whether it's the character or actor, deflating the tension
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection joke deflating dramatic moment with mundane celebrity confusion

jokes.text: Oh, where are my glasses? Oh, they're right on top of my head, for God's sake.
jokes.setup: Maeby playing elderly character Annette
jokes.punchline: Classic 'old person looking for glasses on head' bit
jokes.explanation: Standard elderly person trope but serves character purpose of Maeby's disguise

jokes.text: And honestly, if I wanted a hairless lover, I'd probably go underage rather than overage, you know? What's that? No, I mean, not that I would do it on purpose.
jokes.setup: Maeby complaining about having to sleep with elderly man
jokes.punchline: Accidentally suggesting illegal activity then trying to backtrack
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable escalation typical of the show's boundary-pushing humor

jokes.text: What do you want me to do? You want me to shoot a kid? What kind of monster are you? I'm not sure how I became the monster there
jokes.setup: Discussion about George Michael holding gun to someone's head
jokes.punchline: Maeby escalating to shooting kids and then George Michael becoming the monster
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and role reversal - Maeby suggests something horrible then makes George Michael the bad guy

jokes.text: I've always wanted to be a Keystone Cop. It would be my honor to wear this handlebar mustache in the 2nd of July parade.
jokes.setup: Senior actors being recruited for parade
jokes.punchline: Treating fake mustache in off-brand holiday parade as great honor
jokes.explanation: Absurd enthusiasm for minor parade role captures the desperation of actors

jokes.text: Good-bye to our teeth that make it look like I'm smiling so I don't have to.
jokes.setup: Maeby saying goodbye to her elderly disguise elements
jokes.punchline: Fake teeth as emotional labor substitute - smiling so she doesn't have to feel
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark joke about emotional avoidance through physical props - very Arrested Development philosophy

jokes.text: And you, I'll miss you most of all, neighbor's pain medication. I never even got the chance... Nah, I'll just take you with me.
jokes.setup: Maeby doing sentimental goodbyes like Wizard of Oz
jokes.punchline: Sentimental moment about drugs interrupted by practical decision to steal them
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of classic movie line with drug theft - starts sentimental, ends practical and criminal

jokes.text: Who knows where we get these ideas from, right? Well, you. I got that from you. It's always from you.
jokes.setup: George Michael philosophically wondering about paranoid ideas
jokes.punchline: Immediately blaming Maeby specifically
jokes.explanation: Good structure - philosophical question immediately undercut by specific accusation

jokes.text: Thanks to your mother, I can't even feel your son on my lap.
jokes.setup: George Sr. talking about medical issues
jokes.punchline: Accidentally phrasing inability to feel child in creepy way
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable double entendre from innocent medical complaint

jokes.text: Primos!
jokes.setup: Flashback to cousins going to Mexico
jokes.punchline: Steve Holt's enthusiastic Spanish butchering of 'cousins'
jokes.explanation: Classic Steve Holt enthusiasm with linguistic mangling - very economical character beat

jokes.text: Like how people think the world's melting because people drive cars, or that Hillary should continue to roam free.
jokes.setup: Maeby comparing believing false things
jokes.punchline: Casually dropping climate denial and political conspiracy theories
jokes.explanation: Character revealing political views through casual conspiracy theories - fits her contrarian nature

jokes.text: Good-bye, Fox & Friends.
jokes.setup: Maeby saying goodbye to her elderly lifestyle
jokes.punchline: Revealing she watches Fox News as old person
jokes.explanation: Nice capper connecting her political comments to elderly demographic viewing habits

jokes.text: I will be very surprised, though I may not look it.
jokes.setup: Sally talking about being surprised if Lindsay shows up
jokes.punchline: Self-aware reference to her Botox preventing facial expressions
jokes.explanation: Self-aware character joke about her own physical limitations from plastic surgery

jokes.text: It's on the second this year.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Fourth of July parade
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that July 4th parade is on July 2nd
jokes.explanation: Absurd bureaucratic timing delivered as matter-of-fact information

jokes.text: I don't want to tell you what I had to give the warden to make that happen. I don't want to hear it.
jokes.setup: Lucille mentioning what she gave the warden
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s immediate rejection of TMI
jokes.explanation: Classic setup of sexual implication immediately shut down - good economy

jokes.text: I didn't want to get your hopes up, but I'm in Mexico and I have found Lindsay... to be very stubborn. Oh, damn it, I just did exactly what I said I wouldn't do.
jokes.setup: Tobias calling with good news about finding Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Misleading sentence structure making it sound like he found her, then self-aware correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias misdirection with self-awareness - classic character verbal bumbling

jokes.text: Please tell me you prepared a few 'against her wills.'
jokes.setup: Discussing getting Lindsay back from Mexico
jokes.punchline: Lucille casually suggesting kidnapping options
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark Lucille joke treating kidnapping as contingency planning - perfect character integration and quotable wordplay

jokes.text: Oh, my gosh. I do miss that wit, Mother. It's Mrs. Bluth. I wasn't joking.
jokes.setup: Tobias appreciating what he thinks is Lucille's humor
jokes.punchline: Lucille clarifying she was serious about kidnapping
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up clarifying the darkness of previous joke - Tobias missing the evil while Lucille confirms it

jokes.text: Murphybrown saw an armadillo, men held us up at gunpoint, and I got to dress up as an os...
jokes.setup: Tobias recounting his Mexico adventures
jokes.punchline: Escalating from mundane to dangerous to presumably embarrassing costume, cut off mid-word
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with mysterious cutoff - typical Tobias adventure mix of innocent observation and danger

jokes.text: Nah, I love this place. I'll just tell him it's over, and kick him out.
jokes.setup: Maeby realizing she could manipulate Stan to jump off bridge
jokes.punchline: Deciding against murder in favor of simple breakup
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Maeby choosing practical over murderous solution

jokes.text: I'll give you 500 bucks cash for this youngin' right now.
jokes.setup: Tobias and Murphy working off debt
jokes.punchline: Casual offer to buy child
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about child trafficking delivered casually - fits show's uncomfortable boundary pushing

jokes.text: My son is not for sale. Thanks, Dad.
jokes.setup: Offer to buy Murphy
jokes.punchline: Tobias's moral stand followed by Murphy's surprised gratitude
jokes.explanation: Good father-son moment emerging from absurd circumstances - Murphy's surprise at basic parenting

jokes.text: Lindsay? - Tobias? - DeBrie? No, you were right the first time.
jokes.setup: Tobias thinking he found Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Confusion over identities with DeBrie confirming she's pretending to be Lindsay
jokes.explanation: Good reveal of the Lindsay impersonation plot with efficient dialogue.

jokes.text: I've been pretending to be Lindsay the past few months, on account of Marky's face blind.
jokes.setup: Seeing who appears to be Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Revelation it's DeBrie exploiting face blindness disability
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that recontextualizes previous scenes - exploiting disability for relationship

jokes.text: You should hear him: 'Oh, Lindsay, you've let yourself go.' 'Oh, Lindsay, why'd you stop showering?' 'You're missing some back teeth, there.' Yeah. Just like that.
jokes.setup: DeBrie explaining how Marky treats 'Lindsay'
jokes.punchline: Escalating complaints about appearance with DeBrie's matter-of-fact delivery
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy with escalating insults delivered matter-of-factly - shows both DeBrie's condition and Marky's obliviousness

jokes.text: What do you think of the new stair car? I had to make one, because yours disappeared.
jokes.setup: Sally arriving at parade
jokes.punchline: Built replacement for iconic missing prop to embarrass opponent
jokes.explanation: Good callback to central show element used for political embarrassment

jokes.text: Well, you're no stranger to stuffing tissue places when you don't have the goods.
jokes.setup: Sally's comment about hollow candidate
jokes.punchline: Lucille's brutal comeback about Sally's padding/implants
jokes.explanation: Sharp Lucille comeback with sexual innuendo - good structure and economy

jokes.text: Oh, hello. What do you think of the new stair car? I had to make one, because yours disappeared.
jokes.setup: Sally arriving with her own float
jokes.punchline: Revealing she made a stair car float specifically to highlight the missing original
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Sally's strategic thinking and creates visual comedy.

jokes.text: The sun is nobody's friend.
jokes.setup: Discussion about aging and time
jokes.punchline: Fatalistic statement about sun's effects on appearance
jokes.explanation: Economical character line reflecting Sally's cosmetic concerns and existential dread

jokes.text: Is this because I accidentally choked Ron Howard? Because they already made me pay for that. They chopped your hand off? No, they just downgraded me to a manual.
jokes.setup: Buster asking about punishment for choking Ron Howard
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding about hand chopping vs. manual transmission
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback joke playing on Buster's missing hand with transmission joke - meta reference to show's producer

jokes.text: Are there dressing rooms for the talent?
jokes.setup: Buster learning he's in cage on parade float
jokes.punchline: Treating imprisonment as starring role requiring dressing room
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster delusion - treating humiliation as performance opportunity

jokes.text: Oh, well, at least Buster's being held. Which is more than he had as a child.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Buster's imprisonment
jokes.punchline: Brutal joke about Buster's childhood neglect
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark joke about childhood neglect vs. imprisonment - perfect economy and brutal insight into family dynamics

jokes.text: ♪ At a parade? ♪ ♪ ...ade. ♪ ♪ Being watching the parade. ♪ No. With the curb huggers? No.
jokes.setup: Tobias singing about the excitement of being backstage
jokes.punchline: His terrible, awkward song and immediate rejection
jokes.explanation: Standard Tobias musical theater awkwardness, consistent with character but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, 'cause he'd be, like, 'This is the one place where I'm not embarrassed of my dream.' Hey, no. Never apologize for your dream.
jokes.setup: Murphy making cynical joke about clown dreams
jokes.punchline: Tobias taking it seriously and giving sincere dream encouragement
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Murphy's cynicism vs. Tobias's earnest optimism

jokes.text: Who's Robbie? Oh. Yes, of course. You wouldn't know my brother.
jokes.setup: Son mentioning uncle who taught him to juggle
jokes.punchline: Tobias forgetting he has a brother and awkward recovery
jokes.explanation: Good awkward family moment showing how disconnected Tobias is from his extended family.

jokes.text: This is, uh, Relucto, the apprehensive clown.
jokes.setup: Murphy being reluctant about clowning
jokes.punchline: Tobias creating clown persona based on personality trait
jokes.explanation: Good Tobias invention - turning personality flaw into performance character

jokes.text: How would you like to help me in a double-closet sexuality switch two-hander float illusion, no credit, no money? Oh, and you turn gay. I'll pass.
jokes.setup: Gob desperately needing help with magic trick
jokes.punchline: Terrible offer getting progressively worse, immediate rejection
jokes.explanation: Good escalating terrible offer structure - each element worse than the last

jokes.text: No. Um, hey, how would you like to help me in a double-closet sexuality switch two-hander float illusion, no credit, no money? Oh, and you turn gay. - I'll pass.
jokes.setup: Gob's elaborate pitch for his magic trick
jokes.punchline: Michael's immediate, simple rejection
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Gob's elaborate pitch and Michael's practical response.

jokes.text: So you're saying nothing's worse than the sound of the dog screaming?
jokes.setup: Michael saying Gob's not a good magician
jokes.punchline: Gob's traumatic callback to accidentally sawing dog in half
jokes.explanation: Dark callback to traumatic magic failure - shows Gob's lasting trauma from bad performance

jokes.text: So you're saying nothing's worse than the sound of the dog screaming? No, I just meant you're not a very good magician.
jokes.setup: Reference to past magical disaster
jokes.punchline: Michael's blunt assessment of Gob's abilities
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Michael's characteristic brutal honesty about family members.

jokes.text: It's gonna involve you being put into a small, confined space, and you come out gay. If that's a swipe about me being in prison, I understand it.
jokes.setup: Gob explaining magic trick to Buster
jokes.punchline: Buster interpreting it as comment on prison stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - innocent magic description interpreted through prison lens

jokes.text: It's gonna involve you being put into a small, confined space, and you come out gay. If that's a swipe about me being in prison, I understand it.
jokes.setup: Gob describing his magic trick to Buster
jokes.punchline: Buster interpreting it as a prison joke rather than the actual trick
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Buster's prison experience affecting his interpretation of everything.

jokes.text: Oh, there's a dog.
jokes.setup: Gob trying to have family conversation with Buster
jokes.punchline: Immediately distracted by dog, recalling his trauma
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - two words showing Gob's PTSD from dog incident interrupting family moment

jokes.text: Tobias, and if it isn't... what is it again? - LeTrasha? - Hello, again. Those last few teeth aren't going without a fight, are they?
jokes.setup: Lucille meeting DeBrie disguised as Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Brutal comment about DeBrie's missing teeth
jokes.explanation: Classic Lucille cruelty, perfectly delivered and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: The only acting this one's good at is feigning interest in her drug dealer's hobbies. You do see a lot of aquariums.
jokes.setup: Defending DeBrie's acting abilities
jokes.punchline: Brutal assessment of her only acting skill plus drug dealer detail
jokes.explanation: Good Lucille cruelty with specific drug dealer aquarium detail adding authenticity

jokes.text: Oh, God, no. I can't do this. T-This is... No, no, no. ...why I gave this up. DeBRIE: Shy. - No. No, you can. - No. - No, no, no. Shy. - You can't be this critical.
jokes.setup: DeBrie having a breakdown about performing
jokes.punchline: Tobias's attempt at direction while DeBrie spirals
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing both characters' dysfunction, though somewhat repetitive.

jokes.text: Let's all be perfectly still and see if we can hear them.
jokes.setup: Introduction of Milford Academy marching band
jokes.punchline: Band known for being completely silent, crowd trying to hear nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist callback to school's silence training - crowd listening for nothing

jokes.text: You haven't needed a tissue since Nixon resigned.
jokes.setup: Lucille asking for tissue
jokes.punchline: Michael noting her complete lack of emotions for decades
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific historical reference to characterize Lucille's emotional coldness

jokes.text: I did three years in prison because of Buster. - He can do a few weeks for me.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Buster being in prison
jokes.punchline: Lucille's transactional view of family sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille logic showing her transactional view of family relationships and sacrifice.

jokes.text: He can do a few weeks for me.
jokes.setup: Lucille explaining why Buster should stay in prison
jokes.punchline: Treating prison time as reciprocal favor between family members
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille callousness - treating prison sentences as family obligation exchange

jokes.text: They will be neither seen nor heard next year.
jokes.setup: Silent marching band leaving
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'children should be seen and not heard' with band that's neither
jokes.explanation: Good callback with wordplay on traditional saying adapted to silent band

jokes.text: This is gonna be less humiliating.
jokes.setup: Michael abandoning unicycle plan to chase DA
jokes.punchline: Choosing to run after moving vehicle as less embarrassing option
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment about choosing between humiliating options

jokes.text: Oh, you must be Buster's brother. Hop in. I am Buster's brother. Yes. Oh, so how can I help you? And please make sure to wave.
jokes.setup: Michael approaching the D.A. during the parade
jokes.punchline: The D.A. casually inviting him onto her float while reminding him to wave
jokes.explanation: Nice absurd moment showing how the parade setting makes everything surreal.

jokes.text: Well, the magician seems to be just standing there, doing nothing. Ah... you get used to it.
jokes.setup: Observation about Gob's performance
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of Gob's incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about Gob's consistent failure to perform actual magic.

jokes.text: Plus, there wasn't a fourth of May this year.
jokes.setup: Tony explaining why he missed their May 4th date
jokes.punchline: Claiming the date didn't exist as excuse
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist excuse - Tony's magical thinking applied to calendar reality

jokes.text: Oh, he's an attorney we were forced to use one year and he... I guess he likes them young.
jokes.setup: Gob explaining Bob Loblaw reference
jokes.punchline: Casual accusation of attorney liking underage partners
jokes.explanation: Throwaway dark joke about recurring character - casual treatment of serious accusation

jokes.text: No, it's, uh, tainting each other's brand, but same dif. Dif. Same. - Same dif.
jokes.setup: Correction about 'branding each other's taint'
jokes.punchline: Their awkward attempt to sync up their verbal tics
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing their attempt at connection through shared verbal habits.

jokes.text: And they said, 'No refundo.' And they speak English. They're-they're Mexican, but they are Romneys.
jokes.setup: George Sr. losing land to Mexican family
jokes.punchline: Explaining they're Mexican Romneys who speak English and use fake Spanish
jokes.explanation: Good observation about wealthy family using fake Spanish - Romney family political reference

jokes.text: She's a Muslim. She's disrespecting Muslims. She's a ghost. She's disrespecting ghosts.
jokes.setup: Crowd interpreting DeBrie's covered appearance on float
jokes.punchline: Escalating from religious to supernatural offense interpretations
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from political to supernatural outrage - shows absurdity of projection

jokes.text: Well, I'm not proud of this, but we... we were wearing the Ron Howard/Brian Grazer wigs from the Imagine gift shop.
jokes.setup: George Michael explaining disguise in photo
jokes.punchline: Wearing wigs of the show's actual producers as game costumes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke involving actual show producers as costume elements - very original

jokes.text: Ron and Brian Go to Mexico. Was the name of the game?
jokes.setup: Explaining wearing producer wigs
jokes.punchline: Absurd game concept based on real people's travel itinerary
jokes.explanation: Continuation of meta joke - treating real producers as game characters

jokes.text: What were you playing... Ron and Brian? - Go to Mexico. - What's that? Ron and Brian Go to Mexico. Was the name of the game?
jokes.setup: Michael trying to understand the situation
jokes.punchline: George Michael's matter-of-fact naming of their ridiculous game
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment showing how the cousins' boredom led to elaborate producer cosplay.

jokes.text: It's just not the only one. It's one of a bunch.
jokes.setup: Michael saying honesty is best policy
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining moral absolute with relativism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's moral flexibility when convenient

jokes.text: Help! Please! It's a higher priority emergency! Help! Oh, bullshit you didn't redo these floors. They look amazing.
jokes.setup: Tobias desperately seeking medical help for DeBrie
jokes.punchline: Getting distracted by floor renovation in middle of emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias character moment - vanity about interior design overriding medical emergency

jokes.text: Help! Please! We need a doctor! It's an emergency. Hi. It's us again. Help! Did you redo the floors here?
jokes.setup: Tobias urgently seeking medical help
jokes.punchline: Getting distracted by hospital interior design
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias moment showing his tendency to get distracted by superficial details during crises.

jokes.text: I'm under the impression that if you're carried in they put you immediately on the morphine drip? - That's what we've been told before.
jokes.setup: DeBrie asking about hospital drug protocols
jokes.punchline: Tobias confirming they have experience with this system
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about drug-seeking behavior that fits both characters' histories.
